commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1d477df342958a722d7c484f9a57a18c6a7103eb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional agent harness prewarm hook, a write-scoped Gateway `chat.prewarm_agent_runtime` method, Codex app-server warmup, and TUI startup status/send gating with tests.

PR surface: Source +298, Tests +365. Total +663 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a new startup-prewarm feature. Current main lacks the prewarm identifiers, while the PR body describes the cold-start cost but does not include a live replay of the changed path.
commands.review_followup: This needs maintainer review for the SDK/Gateway contract shape and proof waiver/requirements rather than an autonomous repair lane.

target.title: feat(agents): prewarm selected harness runtimes from TUI startup
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an optional generic `AgentHarness.prewarm` contract plus plugin-sdk exports.
- Add a write-scoped `chat.prewarm_agent_runtime` gateway startup method for runtime warmup.
- Prewarm TUI sessions during initial history load, show `warming runtime`, and block the first send until warmup settles.
- Implement Codex harness prewarm through the shared app-server client using configured app-server options and session-bound auth profile bindings.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` found no actionable correctness issues after fixes.
- Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: `run_b469bd019be2`, lease `cbx_6dc20215636d`
  - Passed: conflict markers, changelog attribution, guarded re-export checks, duplicate coverage, dependency pin guard, package patch guard, core typecheck, core test typecheck, extension typecheck, extension test typecheck.
  - Blocked after that by an unrelated current-main oxlint failure in `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts:589` and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts:593`; this PR does not touch that file.
- Current branch was rebased and pushed to `1d477df342958a722d7c484f9a57a18c6a7103eb` on top of current `origin/main`.
- The unrelated mainline lint blocker is isolated in #86934 with a green Crabbox changed-gate run: `run_5d6352699118`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: first TUI send after a cold Codex app-server startup can absorb runtime launch/auth binding cost; the TUI now starts the selected runtime warmup before the first submit is allowed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw Codex worktree plus Crabbox Linux AWS runner for `pnpm check:changed`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `codex review --base origin/main`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -label openclaw-harness-prewarm-check -shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_b469bd019be2` reached all relevant core/extension typecheck lanes green before hitting the unrelated mainline oxlint failure; #86934 proves that current-main lint blocker is fixed independently with Crabbox run `run_5d6352699118`.

Observed result after fix: prewarm is additive, write-scoped, mixed-version guarded, session-auth aware, and failure-tolerant; history load still completes even if prewarm fails.

What was not tested: a live remote TUI cold-start replay after the final rebase, and a full `pnpm check:changed` finish on this branch after #86934 lands.
target.author: vincentkoc
